headline: PETRONAS paid RM52.3 billion dividend but disclosed zero project details
context: PETRONAS paid a RM52.3 billion dividend to the federal government in 2025 but published no project-level breakdown of how the remaining revenue was allocated internally.

cards.big: PETRONAS handed government RM52.3 billion — but won't show where the rest goes
cards.sub: National oil company's internal spending remains opaque despite public ownership

cards.big: Petroleum Development Act 1974 exempts PETRONAS from public disclosure requirements
cards.sub: 50-year-old legislation designed for different era shields modern spending from scrutiny

cards.big: PETRONAS retained RM98 billion for operations and investments in 2025
cards.sub: Internal allocation of RM98 billion happens without parliamentary or public input

cards.big: Norway's Equinor publishes project-level financial data for every major investment
cards.sub: State oil companies can be transparent — PETRONAS chooses not to be

cards.big: PETRONAS belongs to 33 million Malaysians — but reports to them as if they're outsiders
Should PETRONAS be required to publish project-level spending like Norway's Equinor?
